data.title: Geviti Launches First-to-Market Free, Clinically Backed AI Platform for Health Data Interpretation
data.description: A company called Geviti has introduced a new AI platform in Phoenix that is supported by clinical research. This platform is capable of transforming intricate blood biomarker information into easily understandable and practical health recommendations. The service is being offered for free to users.

data.title: CoStar Data Shows Aire Park Driving New Rental Highs in Leeds
data.description: London - Demand for office space in Leeds is moving south of the river to the East Quarter, based on data from CoStar. The City Core, traditionally the main office location in Leeds, experienced a drop in its share of leasing activity from a five-year average of 80% to 60% in 2025. This shift is attributed to a rising interest from professional and financial services companies in the East Quarter. CoStar, a prominent provider of online real estate services and analytics, highlighted this trend in the property market.

data.title: Fortuna Renews Share Buyback Program
data.description: Fortuna Mining Corp., listed on TSX as FVI and NYSE as FSM, based in Vancouver, British Columbia, disclosed on April 17, 2026, that their Board of Directors has given the green light for the extension of their regular course issuer bid (NCIB). The bid allows for the acquisition of up to five percent of the company's common shares that were outstanding as of April 10, 2026.

data.title: New Poll: Americans Want Trust, Accountability, and Independent Expertise as AI Guardrails
data.description: A new nationwide survey conducted by Fathom reveals strong, bipartisan support for AI regulation based on measurable standards, accountability for negative outcomes, and the involvement of independent experts. The report, titled "AI Governance: What Americans Want," underscores the importance of establishing clear guidelines in the rapidly expanding field of artificial intelligence. The survey, carried out in 2026, highlights the prevalent and enduring call for governance in AI, showcasing the need for verifiable regulations to address potential risks associated with the technology. This data presents the critical need for policymakers to act in response to the public's sentiments, with xx% of respondents expressing a desire for more stringent oversight in the development and application of AI. The survey emphasizes the necessity of implementing ethical frameworks and oversight mechanisms, with xx% of participants advocating for accountability measures to mitigate any harm caused by AI systems. Additionally, the inclusion of independent experts in decision-making processes regarding AI was supported by xx% of those surveyed, indicating a strong consensus on the significance of diverse perspectives in shaping AI governance. The findings underscore the widespread consensus among Americans on the urgency of establishing comprehensive AI governance frameworks to ensure the responsible and beneficial use of this technology.
